judge.rationale: This implementation is severe architectural slop. It does not implement SlidingWindowLimiter at all — there is no class, no constructor, no allow() method, no snapshot() method, and no actual rate-limiting logic. Instead, it attempts to import from a nonexistent 'module_' (which will raise ImportError at runtime), then applies a convoluted Dafny-to-Python symbol aliasing shim that serves no purpose here. The code is maximally indirect, opaque, and non-functional, violating every Zen of Python principle: it is implicit rather than explicit, deeply nested in abstraction, unreadable, and completely fails to preserve task semantics.
